title: Vitest cannot resolve @/ imports because path aliases do not match tsconfig.json
summary: Tests fail with 'Cannot find module @/...' because vitest.config.ts resolve.alias does not mirror tsconfig.json paths.
mistake: Setting up Vitest without replicating the project's tsconfig.json `compilerOptions.paths` in `vitest.config.ts` `resolve.alias`. The agent creates vitest.config.ts with no alias, or with a hardcoded `@` -> `./src` alias without checking what tsconfig.json actually says. Tests then fail with `Cannot find module '@/components/...'` or similar. This is the number one cause of Vitest setup failure in Next.js projects.
instead: Before writing vitest.config.ts, read tsconfig.json `compilerOptions.paths` and replicate EVERY alias entry in `resolve.alias`. For example, if tsconfig has `"@/*": ["./src/*"]`, vitest must have `alias: { '@': path.resolve(__dirname, './src') }`. If tsconfig has additional aliases like `"@db/*": ["./src/db/*"]`, those must also be added. After creating the config, run `npx vitest run` to verify the aliases resolve correctly.
